items.title: Fear of reprisals, isolation, anxiety: Report documents mental health concerns at CSIS
items.summary_auto: Canadian Security Intelligence Service employees who came forward as part of an external study investigating mental health at the spy agency described a sometimes isolating and bleak environment "laden with stigma."

items.title: Health Canada warned against buying peptides online. CBC News easily bought some
items.summary_auto: Companies are selling peptides on the grey market, despite warnings from Health Canada.

items.title: Toronto renters gather this weekend to launch first citywide tenants union
items.summary_auto: Organizers say the new union will unite tenants across Toronto to strengthen collective action on rent, housing conditions, and affordability, while landlords and housing providers also weigh in on the tensions in the rental market.

items.title: Palestinian football officials worry Canada won't approve visas in time for FIFA meeting
items.summary_auto: Media reports published this week suggested Canada had blocked members of the Palestinian Football Association from entering the country ahead of the FIFA Congress in Vancouver later this month. However, it appears that Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) has yet to issue final visa decisions.

items.title: Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz sparks 'tremendous interest' in alternate routes
items.summary_auto: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz during the U.S.-Israel war with Iran has served as wake-up call that a significant source of the world's oil supply remains dependent on the accessibility of this narrow passageway. But, analysts say, it may now force some serious consideration of oil export alternatives.

items.title: The time has come to jump on the Toronto Raptors bandwagon
items.summary_auto: The NBA's first round of playoffs begin Saturday, and for the first time in four years, the Toronto Raptors will be in the fight. And while the pundits may not be calling for Toronto to come out on top,  don't try telling star Scottie Barnes the Raptors are the underdogs.

items.title: Teen's death prompts push to ban energy drink sales to Quebec youth under 16
items.summary_auto: The family of a Quebec teen who died after mixing energy drinks and medication is joined by school groups in calling for a ban on energy drink sales to youth under 16, citing health risks and growing support for tighter rules.

items.title: Murder charges to be withdrawn against alleged poison seller Kenneth Law, lawyer says
items.summary_auto: Crown prosecutors are withdrawing all 14 murder charges laid against an Ontario man accused of selling a toxic substance to people at risk of self-harm, according to his lawyer. In turn, Kenneth Law will plead guilty to counselling or aiding suicide, Matthew Gourlay told CBC News on Friday evening.

items.title: U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick takes a swipe at Canada's trade strategy ahead of review
items.summary_auto: U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick on Friday criticized the current iteration of North America's free trade agreement as a bad deal, taking a swipe at Canada's trade strategy ahead of upcoming negotiations.

items.title: Community shocked after judge dismisses charges against driver who killed 3 people
items.summary_auto: Hundreds of people outside a courtroom expressed shock and dismay Friday after an Ontario judge dismissed dangerous driving causing death charges against a driver who killed three members of a family in a Markham, Ont., crash.

items.title: Outpouring of sympathy at visitation for Quebec pilot killed in N.Y. crash
items.summary_auto: People gathered in Coteau-du-Lac, Que., on Friday to remember Captain Antoine Forest. The 30-year-old was one of two Air Canada pilots killed in March when their aircraft collided with a fire truck on the runway of a New York airport.

items.title: Prince George Cougars fined $1,500 after rowdy crowd disrupts game, throws beer onto ice
items.summary_auto: The Prince George Cougars failed to provide adequate security during a chaotic game against the Penticton Vees, Western Hockey League officials say.

items.title: Baby, toddler dead after house fire in Brazeau County: RCMP
items.summary_auto: A baby and toddler are dead after they were unable to be rescued from a burning  house in Brazeau County, police said.

items.title: Baby dead, toddler missing after house fire southwest of Edmonton: RCMP
items.summary_auto: A baby and toddler are dead after they were unable to be rescued from a burning  house in Brazeau County, police said.

items.title: Stanley Cup playoffs primer: What to know for each first-round series
items.summary_auto: CBC Sports' daily newsletter gets you ready for all eight matchups in the opening round of the NHL playoffs, featuring three Canadian teams.

items.title: Has looksmaxxing guru Clavicular gone too far?
items.summary_auto: Braden Peters, also known as Clavicular online, was recently hospitalized after a suspected overdose. The 20-year-old influencer’s content, based around ‘looksmaxxing’ tips, has garnered attention for how extreme and dangerous it can be.

items.title: 'She'll never be seen': Dean Penney tells involvement in estranged wife's death to fictitious crime boss
items.summary_auto: On Nov. 30, 2023, Dean Penney told a fictitious crime boss of his involvement in the death of his estranged wife, Jennifer Hillier-Penney, exactly seven years prior —  and the steps he took to hide her body.

items.title: Ex-UCP candidate who says she was branded a racist hopes 'truth can be aired in full' at trial
items.summary_auto: Caylan Ford is hoping a Calgary courtroom will deliver her justice seven years after her private Facebook messages were leaked to media outlets, a move she says branded her a white supremacist and devastated her life.

items.title: Ford recalls 144,000 F-150s in Canada due to gearshift issue
items.summary_auto: Safety issue impacts some vehicles with six-speed automatic transmissions made between 2015 and 2017. The issue can cause unexpected downshifting while the vehicle is moving.

items.title: Boeing announces $36M for research and development at Winnipeg facility
items.summary_auto: Boeing plans to spend $36 million on composite aerospace manufacturing research and development projects at its Winnipeg facility, the aerospace company's president announced Friday.

items.title: Canadian mould makers say quiet U.S. tariff tweak loudly impacts their bottom line
items.summary_auto: The new tax levy that came in Apr. 2 applies to the full customs value of metal articles.

items.title: Tegan Quin wins Canada Reads 2026 championing The Cure for Drowning by Loghan Paylor
items.summary_auto: The historical fiction novel won in a 4-1 vote on the final day of the great Canadian book debate.

items.title: Sexual assault charges stayed against Alberta spiritual leader, wife
items.summary_auto: Sexual assault charges against a self-styled spiritual leader and his wife in the Edmonton area have been stayed.

items.title: New B.C. program to 'track and trace' origins of illicit drug supply
items.summary_auto: B.C. officials have announced a new "track and trace" pilot program using technology they say can reveal patterns about the geographic origins and patterns of the illicit drug supply.

items.title: Calls grow for Starmer to resign, as the shadow of Epstein still looms large over 10 Downing St.
items.summary_auto: Calls are growing for Keir Starmer to resign after it was revealed that Peter Mandelson, who was a close friend to the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, failed a security vetting process but was appointed as the U.K's ambassador to the U. S. anyway.

items.title: Celine Dion drops new song Dansons ahead of Paris residency
items.summary_auto: Canadian icon Celine Dion dropped a new single Friday, marking her latest step back into the spotlight. The soft, romantic French-language ballad is called Dansons, meaning "Let’s dance" in English.
